paragraphs.body: Language is a source of connection and identity within my family. For us, our home language does not necessarily mean talking. It might be done in more of a deed than word. Language serves as a vital tool that deepens our relationships and helps us achieve happiness. This could just be something like my mom making my favorite meal because I had a bad day, or my dad washing my car because I had a busy week and could not get around to it. My mom and dad are the roots to a very stable tree.

annotations.text: My mom and dad are the roots to a very stable tree.
annotations.comment: Aha! I see that you're using a metaphor. Interesting move. I still wonder, though, if this is the most effective form? We'll see...
Is there a more intentional intro form that you could use to more effectively engage your audience? Let's talk :)
annotations.context_left: language does not necessarily mean talking. It might be done in more of a deed than word.

paragraphs.body: I remember my parents always had silly sayings growing up, and I was always so confused as to what they meant. I often thought they were quite funny. A few of them being “people who live in glass houses should not throw stones” or “You catch more flies with honey then you do vinegar. But the one that has stuck with me is “You can lead a horse to water, but you cannot make them drink it”. This quote means that you can show someone an amazing opportunity, but it does not mean they will take it. It is impossible to force someone to take an opportunity Growing up, I thought that this quote was funny, but did not give it much thought. However, once if got to high school, my mom repeated the quote, but this time it stuck. I was confused as to what it meant, but I knew it had a greater meaning behind it. Once I found out what this quote meant, I realized that it was all because my parents just wanted me to succeed. Everything my parents did for me was all to help me. They wanted me to grow up be successful, and most importantly be happy. I came to this realization when my dad and I got in a fight one time over him pressuring me to succeed. He said: “I just want what’s best for you”.

paragraphs.body: On the first day of high school, I did not have many friends. However, I was invited to dinner by a group of girls, yet I passed on the opportunity because I was scared to go. Once I told my mom about the situation she repeated the quote. She told me that I had a great opportunity yet missed my chance. She said if you do not take risks in life, you will never succeed. It is only in failure that you truly succeed by learning from your mistakes.

annotations.comment: Aha! Is this the kind of concrete example I asked for earlier? Perhaps this section is just out of logical order for the essay?

paragraphs.body: In some ways I kind of despised my parents for getting a divorce because I felt like all this love they preached was fake. I was a small child at the time so like every little kid does they try to get their parents back together even though deep down you know it will never work. When parents are divorced, some kids may feel like they are lonely or are not loved anymore, for me it is the total opposite. Although my home is split into two now, my parents have never stopped the “home” atmosphere in both houses. I constantly feel that they are both here for me and, I always feel a sense of relief talking to anyone in my immediate family. Even though there are two houses, it is still one big home for me. This language and atmosphere make me feel so comfortable, and it allows me to act like my silly, goofy self in front of them where as I might try to act cool, or not like me around people who I do not trust or I’m not conferrable with. People who do not understand me might think that me being outgoing is weird or not cool, but I know when I am inside my house, I am safe from all that worry. My family is where I feel safe and locked in; other people’s relationships with their families might be similar, but nothing like mine. This love aspect never stopped because of how important my parents know it is to me, it might honestly be a key role in me succeeding when I feel supported by my loved ones. My mom, where to start about everything she has done for me and wants nothing but the best for me. She is my role model and best friend, she gives me a sense of love every single day. Since being at college I’ve realized how much she actually loves me. The first two weeks here I was struggling really bad and couldn’t make friends, was very homesick and honestly overall hated it here. She stayed on the phone with me for hours every day just to make sure I was okay and when she didn’t get to talk she would she me little care packages, again going back to love in my family is sometimes show more in deeds then in words.

annotations.text: In some ways I kind of despised my parents for getting a divorce because I felt like all this love they preached was fake.
annotations.comment: Wow. Interesting turn in the essay. There's a lot of friction, here. I'm compelled by the honesty of this voice, but I'm not yet sure how this sentence relates to the horse quote? I'm excited to explore it :)

annotations.text: again going back to love in my family is sometimes show more in deeds then in words.

I mean, I think you began the essay thinking it was about one thing and then "wrote your way into" a different idea. Let's figure out what's really at the heart of this essay :)

paragraphs.body: My dad, he also has done everything for me, but we are not as close as my mom and I. However, that does not mean that he has not done the same exact thing since I have been away to make sure I’m okay and set up to succeed.

My mom and dad have both told me that I am a hard worker and I get what I want because I work for it. This everyday inspires me to make them proud and makes me want to succeed so I make them proud. The feeling of telling them I studied for 10 hours but then got a 90 on the exam makes me feel happy and rewarded.
My true language is being with my family enjoying being in their time and me feeling loved by all their actions.

To speak in love means knowing secrets about one another, to feel comfortable and secure, through the pain love daily and fully because you don’t know when, it will end, your heart will feel warm and fuzzy, you are the light in love believe
